target.name: Phoenix Tailings
target.founder: Nick Myers (CEO), Tomás Villalón (CTO, Activate Boston fellow), Anthony Balladon

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $118M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Phoenix Tailings is a Series B–stage frontier-tech company in the Energy & Climate sector, founded by Nick Myers (CEO), Tomás Villalón (CTO, Activate Boston fellow), and Anthony Balladon. The company has raised $117.8M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of public comparables and private peer transactions in the available dataset.

The stage-progression method is applicable and provides the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($118M) as a proxy for implied post-money value at prior close, a typical Series B–to–next–stage multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $224M. This method assumes Phoenix Tailings follows sector norms for financing step-ups and that prior rounds reflect reasonable market pricing.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $190M (low) to $257M (high), with a median of $224M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological input; the analysis would benefit from comparable transaction data or revenue-based metrics. Key uncertainties include the applicability of sector-standard step-up multiples to frontier-energy ventures, the company's revenue trajectory and unit economics, and competitive positioning within decarbonization infrastructure. Without public or private peer benchmarks, this range should be treated as indicative pending deeper due diligence on commercial traction and market opportunity.
